response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings 20+ years of broad software engineering experience, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, native-level Japanese proficiency — listed as a non-negotiable requirement — is entirely absent from the resume. Beyond the language barrier, the candidate's career is centered on web, full-stack, and TypeScript/Node.js development with no demonstrated experience in embedded networking firmware, LAN-switch products, or the relevant security protocols (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal). While C/C++ appears briefly in older roles, it is not a sustained focus, and there is no evidence of Linux kernel-level investigation or source modification work.
strengths: 20+ years of software engineering across diverse domains, demonstrating strong adaptability and problem-solving capability
Early career exposure to C/C++ (NI LabWindows CVI, wave field synthesis thesis) and some embedded/robotics work at Altom
Demonstrated experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second)
concerns: Native-level Japanese proficiency is entirely absent — this is a listed hard requirement with no workaround
No experience with embedded networking firmware, LAN-switch products, or security protocols such as MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, or Captive Portal
Core technical stack (TypeScript, Node.js, web full-stack) is orthogonal to the C/embedded firmware development focus of this position

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security protocol expertise
